1-naive.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Breast Cancer Diagnoses (Current & Personal History)

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|160303001&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Query | Code System | Code | Covers |
|-------|-------------|------|--------|
| 1 | SNOMED CT | `254837009` (`:below` for subtypes) | Active/current malignant neoplasm of breast & all subtypes |
| 2 | ICD-10-CM | `C50` (`:below` for subcategories) | C50.0–C50.9 — all anatomical variants of breast malignancy |
| 3 | ICD-10-CM | `Z85.3` | Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast |
| 4 | SNOMED CT | `160303001` | History of malignant neoplasm of breast |

### Notes
- **`:below` modifier** enables hierarchical/subsumption searching, capturing all child codes (e.g., ductal carcinoma, lobular carcinoma, etc.)
- **`_include=Condition:subject`** pulls associated `Patient` resources in the same Bundle
- **Z85.3 / SNOMED 160303001** specifically targets patients with a *prior* breast cancer history (e.g., in remission or resolved)
- You may also add `&clinical-status=active` to Query 1/2 to restrict to *currently active* diagnoses only
1-naive.prompt_text: Find all patients with a diagnosis of breast cancer, including both current diagnoses and personal history of breast cancer.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Breast Cancer Diagnoses (Current & Historical)

### Current/Active Breast Cancer Diagnoses

**SNOMED CT (hierarchical — includes all subtypes):**
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&_include=Condition:patient
```

**ICD-10-CM (hierarchical — covers all C50.x subcategories):**
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Personal History of Breast Cancer

**ICD-10-CM (Z85.3 — Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3&_include=Condition:patient
```

**SNOMED CT (Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|413448000&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Notes

| Query | Code System | Coverage |
|---|---|---|
| `code:below` SNOMED `254837009` | SNOMED CT | Active malignancy + all subtypes (ductal, lobular, etc.) |
| `code:below` ICD-10 `C50` | ICD-10-CM | C50.0–C50.9 all breast malignancy subcategories |
| `Z85.3` | ICD-10-CM | Resolved/historical breast cancer in problem list |
| SNOMED `413448000` | SNOMED CT | Historical breast cancer (SNOMED-coded) |

- `_include=Condition:patient` fetches the associated **Patient** resources in the same bundle
- Use `&clinical-status=active` on the first two queries if you want to explicitly filter to currently active diagnoses
- Personal history is often coded as a **Condition** with `clinicalStatus = resolved` or `verificationStatus = entered-in-error` — consider adding `&clinical-status=resolved` to the first two queries to capture those cases as well

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Breast Cancer Diagnoses (Current & Historical)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|415068001&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Query | Code System | Code | Description |
|-------|------------|------|-------------|
| 1 | SNOMED CT | `254837009` | Malignant neoplasm of breast *(current diagnosis)* |
| 2 | ICD-10-CM | `C50` | Malignant neoplasm of breast *(covers all C50.x subcodes via `:below` subsumption)* |
| 3 | SNOMED CT | `415068001` | Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast |
| 4 | ICD-10-CM | `Z85.3` | Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast |

### Notes
- **`_include=Condition:patient`** retrieves the linked `Patient` resources in the same bundle.
- If your server supports **SNOMED subsumption**, you can append `:below` to the code parameter (e.g., `code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009`) to capture all descendant cancer concepts (e.g., lobular carcinoma, ductal carcinoma, etc.).
- ICD-10-CM `C50` covers all subcategories (`C50.0–C50.9`) for both male and female breast cancer; `Z85.3` captures documented personal history.
- Consider also filtering by `clinicalStatus=active` for **Query 1 & 2** if only confirmed active cases are needed.

## FHIR Query: Female Patients ≥18 with Breast Cancer or History of Breast Cancer

**Date Calculation:** 18 years before `2026-06-02` → birthdate ≤ `2008-06-02`

---

### Active / Current Breast Cancer Diagnosis

**SNOMED CT — Malignant Neoplasm of Breast:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&_include=Condition:subject
```

**ICD-10-CM — C50 Malignant Neoplasm of Breast (common female site codes):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.911,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.912,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.919&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### History / Prior Breast Cancer

**ICD-10-CM — Z85.3 Personal History of Malignant Neoplasm of Breast:**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

**SNOMED CT — Resolved Breast Cancer (clinically inactive):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|resolved&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Patient-Centric Alternative (using `_has`)

```
Patient?gender=female&birthdate=le2008-06-02&_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009
Patient?gender=female&birthdate=le2008-06-02&_has:Condition:patient:code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3
```

---

### Key Notes
| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `birthdate=le2008-06-02` | ≤ 2008-06-02 | Ensures age ≥ 18 as of today |
| `subject:Patient.gender=female` | Chained filter | Restricts to female patients |
| SNOMED `254837009` | Malignant neoplasm of breast | Primary breast cancer |
| ICD-10 `C50.9x1/2` | Right/Left female breast | Common unspecified-site codes |
| ICD-10 `Z85.3` | Personal history of breast cancer | Documented history |
| `_include=Condition:subject` | Returns Patient resources | Enables patient record retrieval |

> ⚠️ Additional ICD-10-CM C50 subcodes (C50.0–C50.8 series) should be added for full coverage of all breast sub-sites.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Female Patients (≥18 yrs) with Breast Cancer Diagnosis or History

**Age cutoff:** Born on or before `2008-06-02` (18 years before current date `2026-06-02`)

---

### Query 1 — Active/Current Breast Cancer (SNOMED CT, hierarchical)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 2 — Active/Current Breast Cancer (ICD-10-CM C50.x, hierarchical)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 3 — Personal History of Breast Cancer (ICD-10-CM Z85.3)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Query 4 — History of Breast Cancer (SNOMED CT)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|416940007&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Notes
| Parameter | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `code:below` | Uses hierarchy to capture all sub-codes (e.g., C50.011, C50.912, etc.) |
| `subject:Patient.gender=female` | Chained parameter to filter only female patients |
| `subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02` | Patients born ≤ 2008-06-02 are ≥ 18 years old as of today |
| `_include=Condition:subject` | Returns matching Patient resources alongside Conditions in the Bundle |
| `Z85.3` | ICD-10-CM code for *Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast* |
| `254837009` | SNOMED CT root for *Malignant neoplasm of breast* |

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries

**Key Parameters Used:**
- Current date: `2026-06-02` → 18 years ago cutoff: `2008-06-02`
- Female gender filter chained to Condition via `subject:Patient`
- Breast cancer codes: SNOMED `254837009`, ICD-10-CM `C50.911`, `C50.912`, `C50.919`
- History of breast cancer: ICD-10-CM `Z85.3`
- Clinical status variants: `active`, `remission`, `resolved`

---

### 1️⃣ Baseline: Female Patients Aged ≥ 18
```
Patient?gender=female&birthdate=le2008-06-02
```

---

### 2️⃣ Active/Current Breast Cancer Diagnosis
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.911,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.912,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.919&clinical-status=active&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 3️⃣ Documented History of Breast Cancer (Z85.3 Code)
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Z85.3&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 4️⃣ Resolved / In Remission Breast Cancer (Historical Conditions)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.911,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.912,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50.919&clinical-status=remission,resolved&subject:Patient.gender=female&subject:Patient.birthdate=le2008-06-02&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Notes
| Query | Coverage |
|-------|----------|
| #2 | Current active breast cancer (SNOMED + ICD-10 C50.x) |
| #3 | Explicitly coded personal history (`Z85.3`) |
| #4 | Past breast cancer with resolved/remission clinical status |
| `_include=Condition:subject` | Returns matching Patient resources alongside Conditions |

> ⚠️ ICD-10-CM C50 has many sub-codes (C50.011–C50.929). Servers supporting **FHIR terminology subsumption** can replace individual C50 codes with a single `C50` parent code search. Additionally, adapt SNOMED codes to include site-specific subtypes if needed (e.g., `372244006` for female breast specifically).

## Breast Cancer Population Identification Queries

---

### 🏷️ GROUP 1 — Breast Cancer Diagnosis Conditions

**1A · ICD-10-CM — Full C50 hierarchy (`:below` for all anatomic sub-sites)**
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50
```

**1B · SNOMED CT — Parent malignancy (`:below` captures IDC, ILC, carcinoma NOS subtypes)**
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009
```

**1C · SNOMED CT — Explicit histology codes (fallback for servers that don't support `:below`)**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009,http://snomed.info/sct|413448000,http://snomed.info/sct|32968003,http://snomed.info/sct|722524005,http://snomed.info/sct|372137005
```

---

### 💊 GROUP 2A — Endocrine Therapy (SERM + Aromatase Inhibitors)

**2A-i · Tamoxifen** *(HR+ adjuvant therapy AND chemoprevention — noted source of overcounting)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493
```

**2A-ii · Aromatase Inhibitors — anastrozole + letrozole + exemestane (combined OR query)**
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72965,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|195694
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72965,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|195694
```

---

### 💉 GROUP 2B — Anti-HER2 Therapy (Trastuzumab)

**2B · Trastuzumab** *(IV biologic — check both prescription and administration records)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|224905
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|224905
MedicationAdministration?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|224905
```

---

### 📋 Code Reference Table

| Query | Code | System | Description |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|
| 1A | `C50` + `:below` | ICD-10-CM | All breast malignancy sub-sites |
| 1B/1C | `254837009` | SNOMED CT | Malignant neoplasm of breast (parent) |
| 1C | `413448000` | SNOMED CT | Invasive ductal carcinoma (IDC) |
| 1C | `32968003` | SNOMED CT | Infiltrating lobular carcinoma (ILC) |
| 1C | `722524005` | SNOMED CT | Invasive carcinoma of breast (general) |
| 1C | `372137005` | SNOMED CT | Carcinoma of breast NOS |
| 2A-i | `41493` | RxNorm | Tamoxifen *(SERM — also used for chemoprevention)* |
| 2A-ii | `84857` | RxNorm | Anastrozole (AI) |
| 2A-ii | `72965` | RxNorm | Letrozole (AI) |
| 2A-ii | `195694` | RxNorm | Exemestane (AI) |
| 2B | `224905` | RxNorm | Trastuzumab (anti-HER2) |

---

### ⚠️ Deduplication & Population Notes

| Concern | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Overcounting from tamoxifen/AIs** | Cross-reference MedicationRequest results against Group 1 Condition results; patients with medication only and **no** C50/breast malignancy code are likely chemoprevention cases |
| **Survivorship (resolved diagnoses)** | Queries above include all `Condition.clinicalStatus` values; do **not** filter on `clinical-status=active` if you want the full survivorship cohort |
| **`:below` server support** | If your server does not support `:below` for ICD-10-CM or SNOMED, use Query 1C as the explicit-code fallback |
| **Trastuzumab administration gaps** | Biologic infusions are often recorded in `MedicationAdministration` rather than `MedicationRequest`; the 2B triad covers all three resources |
| **Patient union** | Collect `subject.reference` (patient IDs) from all result bundles and deduplicate to form the final cohort |

1-broad.raw_response:
# Breast Cancer Population Identification — FHIR Queries

---

## 🩺 Group 1: Breast Cancer Diagnosis Queries

### Query 1 — ICD-10-CM Hierarchical (`:below` on parent C50)
Captures all laterality, quadrant, and site subcodes (C50.011–C50.919) via subsumption:
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Query 2 — SNOMED Subsumption (Parent: Malignant neoplasm of breast `254837009`)
Traverses the SNOMED hierarchy to capture IDC, ILC, invasive carcinoma, and all other breast malignancy descendants:
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Query 3 — Explicit SNOMED Codes *(Fallback: servers without `:below` support)*
Belt-and-suspenders approach listing the five key concepts individually:

| SNOMED Code | Concept |
|-------------|---------|
| `254837009` | Malignant neoplasm of breast *(parent)* |
| `372046006` | Invasive carcinoma of breast |
| `413448000` | Infiltrating duct carcinoma of breast *(IDC)* |
| `32968003` | Infiltrating lobular carcinoma of breast *(ILC)* |
| `372137005` | Primary malignant neoplasm of breast |

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009,http://snomed.info/sct|372046006,http://snomed.info/sct|413448000,http://snomed.info/sct|32968003,http://snomed.info/sct|372137005&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

## 💊 Group 2: Adjuvant / Chemoprevention Medication Queries

### Query 4 — Tamoxifen *(SERM; HR+ adjuvant & chemoprevention)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 5 — Aromatase Inhibitors *(anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane)*

| RxNorm | Drug |
|--------|------|
| `84857` | Anastrozole (Arimidex) |
| `72251` | Letrozole (Femara) |
| `195694` | Exemestane (Aromasin) |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72251,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|195694&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 6 — Trastuzumab, MedicationRequest *(HER2+ oral/SC formulations & prescriptions)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|224905&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Query 7 — Trastuzumab, MedicationAdministration *(IV infusion administration records)*
IV infusions are rarely in MedicationRequest; this captures infusion center administrations:
```
MedicationAdministration?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|224905&_include=MedicationAdministration:patient
```

---

### Query 8 — MedicationStatement, All Agents *(patient-reported, legacy, or reconciled records)*
```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72251,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|195694,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|224905&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

---

## 🔗 Union Strategy & Deduplication

```
Final Population = UNION(Q1 ∪ Q2 ∪ Q3) ∪ (Q4 ∪ Q5 ∪ Q6 ∪ Q7 ∪ Q8)
Deduplicate on: Patient.id
```

After retrieving results, deduplicate using the Patient resource `id` returned via `_include`. A patient appearing in any one query is included in the final cohort.

---

## ⚠️ Caveats & Operational Notes

| Issue | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Chemoprevention overcounting** | Queries 4–5 (tamoxifen/AIs) will include patients on chemoprevention without a C50/SNOMED diagnosis. Cross-reference Group 1 to identify the ~5–10% chemoprevention-only subset for exclusion or separate analysis. |
| **`:below` server support** | Queries 1–2 require FHIR terminology service subsumption. If your server lacks this, rely on Query 3 (explicit SNOMED) and expand the ICD-10-CM list manually to C50.x11/x12/x19 laterality variants. |
| **Trastuzumab biosimilars** | RxNorm `224905` is the reference product. Biosimilars (trastuzumab-dkst, -pkrb, -anns, -qyyp, -dttb) carry separate RxNorm CUIs; extend Query 6–7 with those codes if biosimilar penetration is significant in your formulary. |
| **Medication coding pattern** | These queries use the `code` search parameter, which resolves `medication[x]` as a CodeableConcept. For EHRs that store `medicationReference`, add a chained query: `MedicationRequest?medication:Medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm\|10324` etc. |
| **Survivorship vs. active-only** | `verification-status=confirmed` is applied; `clinical-status` is intentionally **not** filtered so that patients in remission or with resolved conditions are included in the survivorship cohort. |
| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=1000` (or server max) and iterate `Bundle.link[relation=next]` for large populations. |

## 🔬 Discovery Summary

### Server Profile
- **FHIR R4** (Azure FHIR Server 4.0.770) — **no IGs loaded**, so no US Core category constraints needed
- **Condition coding:** Triple-coded on every record — SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously (confirmed by sampling 5 records)
- **MedicationRequest coding:** RxNorm only (clinical drug formulation codes); all sampled meds are `category=community` (outpatient oral drugs)
- **Injectable drugs (trastuzumab) return 0** — this server captures only oral/outpatient prescriptions; IV biologics administered in clinic are not in MedicationRequest here

### SNOMED Hierarchy (from subsumption checks)
| Code | Display | Relationship |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `372137005` | Primary malignant neoplasm of breast | Separate branch — must include explicitly |
| `254838004` | Carcinoma of breast | **Parent of IDC** (`408643008`) via subsumption |
| `408643008` | Infiltrating duct carcinoma of breast (IDC) | Subsumed by `254838004` — included explicitly for clarity |
| `278054005` | Lobular carcinoma of breast (ILC) | Separate branch from `254838004` — must include explicitly |

> ⚠️ `372137005` (Primary malignant neoplasm) is **not** a subsumption parent of `254838004` (Carcinoma of breast) — they sit in different SNOMED CT branches. Both must be listed.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:

## 🔬 Discovery Summary

### Server Profile
- **FHIR 4.0.1** — Azure FHIR Server, **no IGs loaded** (no US Core); standard FHIR R4 coding rules apply
- **Conditions** are triple-coded: **SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM** on every record (confirmed in 5/5 samples)
- **MedicationRequests** are coded with **RxNorm clinical-drug-form codes only** (e.g., `exemestane 25 MG Oral Tablet`, not ingredient-level)

| ICD-10-CM | `C50.919` (dominant), `C50.912`, `C50.911` | 465 records (superset) |
| ICD-9-CM | `174.9` (dominant), `174.6` | Co-coded on same resources |
| **Key finding** | **C50.3xx–C50.8xx returned 0 results** — server uses "unspecified site" C50.9xx codes heavily. SNOMED (306) is a subset of the ICD-10 (465) records; ~159 records have ICD-10/ICD-9 only. **Query 2 below captures the dominant population; Query 1 is for completeness and SNOMED-only records.** | |

| Anastrozole (AI) | NCQA Aromatase Inhibitors (active) | `199224` | Included in 274 |
| Letrozole (AI) | NCQA Aromatase Inhibitors (active) | `200064` | Included in 274 |

| **Tamoxifen + AIs Total** | | **9 codes** | **274 prescription records** ✅ |
| Trastuzumab (reference + biosimilars) | ASCO "Trastuzumab and Biosimilars" (active 2026) | 17 codes: `1922509`, `2107053`, `2119714`, `2169359`, `2174804`, `2179753`, `2179760`, `2257297`, `2280732`, `2289227`, `2289234`, `2472791`, T-DM1: `1658084`/`1658089`, T-DXd: `2267576`, Phesgo: `2382604`/`2383305` | **0 records on this server** |

---

## Discovery Summary

### Server Architecture
| Dimension | Finding |
|-----------|---------|
| FHIR version | R4 (Azure FHIR Server 4.0.770) |
| IGs loaded | None — no US Core, no ONC profiles |
| Condition coding | **Tri-coded**: SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously on every record |
| MedicationRequest coding | **RxNorm-only** — product-level for small molecules, **ingredient-level** for biologics |

---

### Query 1 — Breast Cancer Diagnosis (Condition)

**Code intelligence gathered:**
- VSAC MITRE "Breast Cancer" (OID .366): **66 ICD-10-CM codes** — full C50.0xx → C50.9xx matrix (all anatomical sites × lateralities) + D05.x DCIS
- VSAC MITRE "Breast Cancer" (OID .367 / .365): **185–251 SNOMED codes** — IDC variants, ILC variants, general carcinoma, lobular, scirrhous, metaplastic, inflammatory, in situ, and bilateral
- Server samples confirmed 3 active SNOMED codes: `408643008` (IDC), `254838004` (Carcinoma of breast), `278054005` (ILC/Lobular)
- ICD-9-CM legacy codes 174.x / 175.x confirmed populated on server

**Count validation:**
| Code system / set | Count |
|---|---|
| SNOMED 9-code set (IDC + carcinoma + ILC + primaries + DCIS) | **306** records |
| ICD-10-CM 30-code set (all C50.x female breast sites) | **465** records ✅ |
| ICD-9-CM 174.x/175.x (legacy) | **465** records (same cohort, co-coded) |
| ICD-10-CM male + inflammatory + DCIS codes | 0 (not populated on this server) |

→ **ICD-10-CM is the dominant system** — captures 159 more records than SNOMED alone because some records use SNOMED subtypes beyond the 3 seen in samples. Use both systems to maximize recall.

---

### Query 2 — Endocrine Therapy (MedicationRequest)

| Drug | RxNorm | Count contribution | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tamoxifen 10 mg oral tablet | `198240` | ✅ confirmed on server | PCPI VSAC |
| Tamoxifen 20 mg oral tablet | `313195` | ✅ confirmed on server | PCPI VSAC |
| Tamoxifen 2 mg/mL oral solution | `389230` | included for completeness | PCPI VSAC |
| Anastrozole 1 mg oral tablet | `199224` | ✅ confirmed on server | AIR/PCPI VSAC |
| Letrozole 2.5 mg oral tablet | `200064` | ✅ confirmed on server | AIR/PCPI VSAC |
| Exemestane 25 mg oral tablet | `310261` | ✅ confirmed on server | AIR/PCPI VSAC |
| Letrozole+ribociclib pack (3 variants) | `1923053`, `1923055`, `1923057` | CDK4/6+AI combo | AIR VSAC |

**Total endocrine therapy MedicationRequests: 274** ✅

---

### Query 3 — HER2-directed Therapy / Trastuzumab (MedicationRequest)

**Critical finding — server uses ingredient-level RxNorm, not VSAC product codes:**
- ASCO "Trastuzumab Injectable" VSAC set (product-level codes like `1922509`, `2179753`) → **0 results**
- `224905` = **trastuzumab** (RxNorm ingredient-level) → **63 results** ✅ — confirmed by `vsac_lookup_code` + live sample

| Drug | RxNorm | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| trastuzumab (ingredient) | `224905` | All reference/biosimilar records use this ingredient code |

3-naive.raw_response:
ICD-9 codes add no additional records (those patients already captured by SNOMED/ICD-10 codings). Both queries are fully validated. Now I can present the comprehensive results.

---

## 🎯 Breast Cancer Survivorship Cohort — Query Results

### Methodology Applied
- **Playbook 1 (Subtypes)**: Umbrella `Primary malignant neoplasm of breast` + specific histologies (IDC, ILC, Carcinoma of breast)
- **Playbook 8 (Multi-system code lists)**: SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM in a single comma-separated `code=` parameter per query
- **Playbook 12 (Cohort = OR union)**: Two query lines that the harness unions across evidence sources

---

### What Was Found During Discovery

| Finding | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Server code systems** | Conditions use SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM (all present on each resource) |
| **Medications** | RxNorm product-level codes for AIs; ingredient-level codes for tamoxifen & trastuzumab |
| **ICD-10 site-specificity** | C50.0xx–C50.5xx codes (site-specific quadrant) return **0** in this dataset; only C50.6xx–C50.9xx ("axillary tail", "overlapping", "unspecified site") are actually used |
| **SNOMED codes confirmed** | `372137005` Primary malignant neoplasm of breast, `254838004` Carcinoma of breast, `408643008` IDC, `278054005` Lobular carcinoma |
| **ICD-9 adds nothing** | 174.9 and 174.6 are co-present on the same records already captured by SNOMED/ICD-10 |
| **AI ingredient codes fail** | `84857` (anastrozole), `258494` (exemestane) → 0 results; server indexes AIs at product level |
| **Tamoxifen + trastuzumab** | Ingredient codes `10324` and `224905` work correctly |

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete breast cancer patient population for a treatment adherence and survivorship quality initiative. From clinical experience, I know breast cancer patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal diagnosis code with histology (IDC, ILC, or general carcinoma), and most receive adjuvant systemic therapy (endocrine therapy for HR-positive disease, trastuzumab for HER2-positive disease). A small number of patients receive tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors for chemoprevention without an active breast cancer diagnosis.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any breast cancer diagnosis (parent malignant neoplasm, carcinoma of breast, IDC, ILC, or invasive carcinoma)
2. Patients prescribed adjuvant therapy (tamoxifen, aromatase inhibitors, or trastuzumab)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found. Note that the medication query will overcount slightly because of the chemoprevention population.

3-expert.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete breast cancer patient population for a treatment adherence and survivorship quality initiative. From clinical experience, I know breast cancer patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal diagnosis code with histology (IDC, ILC, or general carcinoma), and most receive adjuvant systemic therapy (endocrine therapy for HR-positive disease, trastuzumab for HER2-positive disease). A small number of patients receive tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors for chemoprevention without an active breast cancer diagnosis.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any breast cancer diagnosis (parent malignant neoplasm, carcinoma of breast, IDC, ILC, or invasive carcinoma)
2. Patients prescribed adjuvant therapy (tamoxifen, aromatase inhibitors, or trastuzumab)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found. Note that the medication query will overcount slightly because of the chemoprevention population.

Here are FHIR queries to find all patients with breast cancer using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM coding systems:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&_include=Condition:subject
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50
```

### Notes:
| Query Approach | Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Condition + `_include` | SNOMED CT | `254837009` | Malignant neoplasm of breast |
| Condition + `_include` | ICD-10-CM | `C50` | Malignant neoplasm of breast (parent code) |
| Patient via `_has` | SNOMED CT | `254837009` | Returns Patient resources directly |
| Patient via `_has` | ICD-10-CM | `C50` | Returns Patient resources directly |

- The **`_include=Condition:subject`** queries return both `Condition` and linked `Patient` resources in one bundle.
- The **`_has`** queries return only `Patient` resources with a matching condition.
- If your server supports the **`:below`** modifier, you can use `code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50:below` to capture all subcodes (e.g., `C50.1`, `C50.911`, etc.).
- Consider combining results from both code systems to ensure complete coverage across different data sources.

This requires **two-phase querying**: (1) find patients on target medications, then (2) identify patients with a breast cancer diagnosis — the answer is **Set 1 minus Set 2**.

---

### Phase 1 — Patients on Tamoxifen or Aromatase Inhibitors

```
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72965&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationRequest?medication=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|195878&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Phase 2 — Patients WITH Breast Cancer Diagnosis (to exclude)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Drug Code Reference

| Drug | RxNorm | Class |
|---|---|---|
| Tamoxifen | `10324` | SERM |
| Anastrozole | `84857` | Aromatase Inhibitor |
| Letrozole | `72965` | Aromatase Inhibitor |
| Exemestane | `195878` | Aromatase Inhibitor |

### Diagnosis Code Reference

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `254837009` | Malignant neoplasm of breast |
| ICD-10-CM | `C50` | Malignant neoplasm of breast (category root) |

> ⚠️ **Post-processing required**: FHIR has no native cross-resource NOT operator. Collect `subject` references from Phase 1, then subtract any patient IDs returned in Phase 2. 1-broad.raw_response:
## Strategy: Two-Step Identification (Medications → Exclude Breast Cancer Dx)

Since FHIR does not natively support exclusion (NOT) logic in a single query, this requires two steps:
**Step 1** — Find patients on these medications. **Step 2** — Retrieve patients *with* a breast cancer diagnosis (to subtract from Step 1 results client-side).

---

### Step 1 — Active Medication Orders/Records

**Tamoxifen** (RxNorm: `10324`) **+ Aromatase Inhibitors:**
- Anastrozole (RxNorm: `84857`)
- Letrozole (RxNorm: `72965`)
- Exemestane (RxNorm: `44506`)

```
MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72965,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|44506&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationStatement?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|84857,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72965,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|44506&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

---

### Step 2 — Retrieve Breast Cancer Diagnoses to **Exclude**

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009&clinical-status=active&_elements=subject

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C50&clinical-status=active&_elements=subject
```

---

### Notes
| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Exclusion logic** | Subtract `Condition.subject` patient IDs (Step 2) from MedicationRequest/Statement patient IDs (Step 1) |
| **Breast cancer SNOMED** | `254837009` = Malignant neoplasm of breast |
| **ICD-10 C50** | Broad category covering all C50.x breast malignancy codes |
| **Use case** | Remaining patients likely on chemoprevention, off-label use, or missing diagnosis coding |
| **Status filters** | `active` used for both medications and conditions — adjust if historical records needed |

## FHIR Queries for Chemoprevention Population (Inclusion + Exclusion Sets)

> ⚠️ **FHIR has no native NOT/EXCEPT operator.** The chemoprevention cohort = **Set A minus Set B** (application layer computes the set difference).

---

### 🟢 Set A — INCLUSION: Patients with Chemoprevention MedicationRequests

**Patient-centric (preferred for cohort building):**
```
Patient?_has:MedicationRequest:subject:code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|199224,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|200064,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|310261
```

**MedicationRequest detail retrieval (with Patient included):**
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|199224,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|200064,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|310261&status=active,on-hold,completed&_include=MedicationRequest:subject&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

---

### 🔴 Set B — EXCLUSION: Patients with Breast Cancer Conditions

**Patient-centric (returns IDs to subtract from Set A):**
```
Patient?_has:Condition:subject:code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009,http://snomed.info/sct|254838004,http://snomed.info/sct|408643008,http://snomed.info/sct|278054005,http://snomed.info/sct|713609000
```

**Condition detail retrieval (verify active/recurrence status):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254837009,http://snomed.info/sct|254838004,http://snomed.info/sct|408643008,http://snomed.info/sct|278054005,http://snomed.info/sct|713609000&clinical-status=active,recurrence,relapse&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 🔵 Supplemental — Risk Context Conditions (BRCA/Atypical Hyperplasia)

**High-risk genetic markers and precursor lesions (contextual enrichment):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|445180002,http://snomed.info/sct|413448000,http://snomed.info/sct|72984004,http://snomed.info/sct|450426006&_include=Condition:subject
```

**Genetic observations (BRCA1/BRCA2 pathogenic variants via LOINC):**
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|21725-0,http://loinc.org|21726-8&value-concept=http://loinc.org|LA6668-3&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Logic Summary

| Step | Action | Result |
|------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Run **Set A** queries | All patients with tamoxifen/AI prescriptions |
| 2 | Run **Set B** query | All patients with breast cancer diagnosis |
| 3 | `Set A` ∖ `Set B` | **Chemoprevention-only population** |
| 4 | Cross-reference supplemental | Tag BRCA/AH/family-history subgroups |

> **Drug codes summary:** Tamoxifen `10324`, Anastrozole `199224`, Letrozole `200064`, Exemestane `310261` (all RxNorm)
> **Exclusion codes summary:** Malignant neoplasm of breast `254837009`, Carcinoma in situ `254838004`, HER2+ `408643008`, Inflammatory `278054005`, Triple-negative `713609000` (all SNOMED CT)

2-expert.raw_response:
All three code systems (SNOMED, ICD-10-CM, ICD-9-CM) converge to exactly **465 records** — the server triple-codes every Condition, which means all systems are fully interchangeable here. This gives me complete confidence in the final query design.

## What was found

| Check | Finding |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (Azure), no Implementation Guides, standard search params |
| **MedicationRequest coding** | RxNorm only — all 4 drug codes confirmed live (tamoxifen 67, anastrozole ✓, letrozole ✓, exemestane ✓ → **341 total**) |
| **Condition coding** | Triple-coded: SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM on every record |
| **SNOMED-only exclusion count** | 465 breast cancer Conditions |
| **ICD-10-CM-only exclusion count** | 465 ← same set (co-coded) |
| **ICD-9-CM-only exclusion count** | 465 ← same set (co-coded) |
| **VSAC validation** | MITRE Breast Cancer value set (66 codes): full C50.xxx + male variants + D05 DCIS; ASCO set (27 codes): female C50.xxx only |

## Query design (negation pattern)

Since FHIR has no `NOT EXISTS`, this is a **two-query set-difference** pattern:
1. **INCLUSION** — run `MedicationRequest` query → collect `subject` patient IDs
2. **EXCLUSION** — run `Condition` query → collect `subject` patient IDs
3. **Chemoprevention cohort** = Inclusion − Exclusion (client-side)

The exclusion uses **all three code systems** across the full VSAC C50.xxx family (including male breast variants from the MITRE set) + ICD-9 legacy codes, ensuring no breast-cancer patient slips through:

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10324,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|199224,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|200064,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|310261

**Key design decisions:**

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **Playbook 10 (Negation)** | "Without" requires two separate queries; the harness subtracts patient IDs in Query B from those in Query A |
| **Playbook 5 (Iatrogenic/Drug primary)** | Drug exposure is the primary signal — patients are flagged via MedicationRequest first |
| **6 RxNorm drug codes** | Covers all tamoxifen forms (10mg, 20mg, oral solution) + all 3 aromatase inhibitors (anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane) — sourced from PCPI VSAC value set, confirmed live on server |
| **Tri-system condition codes** | Server stores SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM on every Condition; all 3 systems included so legacy records (174.9) are captured |
| **D05.xx included** | Carcinoma in situ of breast — clinically appropriate subtract target for this drug class |

3-expert.raw_response:
All codes confirmed:
- **713609000** = "Invasive carcinoma of breast (disorder)" ✓
- **SNOMED subtract set**: 465 breast cancer Conditions — stable, complete
- **MedicationRequest keep set**: 341 chemopreventive agent prescriptions (tamoxifen 67, plus anastrozole/letrozole/exemestane)
- **ICD-10-CM** parity confirmed (same 465 patients) — SNOMED covers all records

The server dual-codes Conditions in SNOMED + ICD-10-CM on the same record, so SNOMED alone is sufficient and complete for the subtract set. The two Playbook 10 negation queries are ready.

| Role | Query | Count |
|---|---|---|
| **KEEP** (has chemopreventive Rx) | `MedicationRequest?code=RxNorm\|10324,199224,200064,310261` | **341** |
| **SUBTRACT** (has breast cancer Dx) | `Condition?code=SNOMED\|254837009,254838004,408643008,278054005,713609000` | **465** |

The harness takes patients in KEEP minus patients in SUBTRACT → **chemoprevention cohort (no active malignancy)**.
